title: Retire in the Wrong Year and Lose $1M
hook: Two people retire the same month with identical savings — one runs out in sixteen years, the other never does. The only difference is when the market crashed.
angle: Sequence of returns risk — the fatal flaw most retirement calculators hide. The order market returns happen matters as much as the average return itself, and most people have never heard of it.
audience: Anyone planning retirement or thinking about early retirement who relies on the 4% rule or standard calculators
key_numbers: 2000 retiree with $1M at 4% ran out by 2016 — only 16 years
The 4% rule is 95% reliable over 30 years — but only 56% over 40+
A 1-year delay at the wrong time can cost $400K+
